differentials.name: Hunter syndrome less severe changes than in Hurler syndrome
differentials.raw: Hunter syndrome less severe changes than in Hurler syndrome

context: √  rounded appearance of vertebral bodies √  mild kyphotic curve with smaller vertebral body at apex of kyphosis displaying tonguelike beak at anterior half (usually at T12 / L1) √  "step-off" deformities along anterior margins √  flattened + widened vertebral bodies √  anterior "tonguelike" elongation of central portion of vertebral bodies √  small flat vertebral bodies √  anterior "tonguelike" deformity (in children only) √  widened disk spaces + irregular endplates
